examples: To disfigure the mouth—this is how I would interpret it—to disfigure the mouth has a primary effect in rendering one at least temporarily speechless.
They use an axe and disfigure the rhino's face.
These diseases blind, maim, disfigure, debilitate, and disable.
"Those who committed these acts disfigure our church, hurt Christian communities, and extend suspicion on all members of the clergy, " the French bishops wrote.
Mary Konye, 21, has been charged with throwing or casting a corrosive fluid with intent to burn, maim, disfigure, disable or do grievous bodily harm.
